justifications.ip1: All three ACs have distinct, concrete failing test scenarios: T1 asserts 'status: ACCEPTED' for AC1, T2 asserts 'status: REJECTED / reason: SCHEME_SLA_EXCEEDED' fired before 10,000ms for AC2, and T3/T4 assert receiptTimestamp, ackTimestamp, and elapsedMs field presence for AC3.
justifications.ip2: No task touches Fraud vendor API, outbound RTP, AML load testing, or batch reconciliation (the DoR's 'What will NOT be built' list); the plan stays strictly within the Contract Proposal scope of wrapping handleInboundMessage with the SLA timer.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files (e.g. 'tests/payments/rtp-sla-timer.test.js', 'src/payments/rtp-sla-timer.js', 'src/payments/rtp-message-handler.js'), provides concrete function names ('wrapWithSlaTimer'), and includes exact assertion values (e.g. 'calls[0].firedAt < 10_000').
justifications.ip4: Task 1 explicitly creates the test file before any implementation exists and confirms 'All four tests fail (module not found or assertion errors) — RED confirmed'; Task 2 then implements and confirms GREEN; Task 4 explicitly labels a RED step before the GREEN step.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 has a dedicated performance task (Task 4, file 'rtp-sla-timer.perf.test.js') asserting P99 < 9,000ms at 40 tps; C3 is enforced as a named constant 'const SLA_THRESHOLD_MS = 9_500' with a HARD SCHEME RULE comment explicitly stating 'NOT configurable'.

notes: Exemplary plan: all ACs have specific failing test assertions, C3 is a named non-configurable constant with scheme-rule commentary, NFR-1 has a real-timer P99 performance test, and every task names exact files and function signatures — no weaknesses detected.
